issues.title: `toolName cannot be null or empty` in  Flux
issues.body:
**Bug description**
I found an issue when making MCP calls, where an error occurs when the requesting controller returns Flux<> in reactive mode
```java
java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: toolName cannot be null or empty
	at org.springframework.util.Assert.hasText(Assert.java:253) ~[spring-core-6.2.3.jar:6.2.3]
	Suppressed: reactor.core.publisher.FluxOnAssembly$OnAssemblyException: 
```

**Environment**
java: oracle openJDK17
Spring AI: 1.0.0-M6
LM Studio: 0.3.12
Model: Qwen2.5

**Steps to reproduce**
```shell
cd spring-mcp-test
git checkout EmptyToolName
mvn clean package
java -jar mcp-server/target/mcp-server.jar
java -jar mcp-client/target/mcp-client.jar
```
```shell
curl http://localhost:8080/ai/mcp?message=Get%scurrent%sDate
# or message=List the files in the UV directory of drive D
```

**Expected behavior**
Some information about files.

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**
Check out the project [https://github.com/fxz26284407/spring-mcp-test](https://github.com/fxz26284407/spring-mcp-test)

comments.body:
I tried running the example file you posted and it returns a message like this

```
What specific help do you need regarding listing files? Please provide more details.%
```

No errors occurred

I tested it with the `gpt-4o-mini` model of Open AI

> I tried running the example file you posted and it returns a message like this
> 
> ```
> What specific help do you need regarding listing files? Please provide more details.%
> ```
> 
> No errors occurred
> 
> I tested it with the `gpt-4o-mini` model of Open AI

Sorry, the message parameter of the URL was written incorrectly. It should be like this
```shell
curl http://localhost:8080/ai/mcp?message=Get%scurrent%sDate
# or message=List the files in the UV directory of drive D
```

@fxz26284407 I've checked out your project, updated it to latest spring-ai 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT
within the EmptyToolName branch I started the server and the client apps (using gpt-4o)

Then thit the http://localhost:8080/ai/mcp?message=Get%scurrent%sDate and got
`What specific directory would you like to list the files from?`

No errors

@tzolov thanks for your reply . I switched the version from M6 to SNAPSHOT, but the problem still persists.I have already pushed the updated code to the [EmptyToolName](https://github.com/fxz26284407/spring-mcp-test/tree/EmptyToolName) branch. Please check it out again and try running it.

```java
2025-03-14T10:14:12.671+08:00 ERROR 15632 --- [mcp-test-client] [oundedElastic-4] o.s.ai.chat.model.MessageAggregator      : Aggregation Error

java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: toolName cannot be null or empty
	at org.springframework.util.Assert.hasText(Assert.java:253) ~[spring-core-6.2.3.jar:6.2.3]
	Suppressed: reactor.core.publisher.FluxOnAssembly$OnAssemblyException: 
Assembly trace from producer [reactor.core.publisher.FluxDefer] :
	reactor.core.publisher.Flux.defer(Flux.java:810)
	org.springframework.ai.openai.OpenAiChatModel.lambda$internalStream$11(OpenAiChatModel.java:351)
Error has been observed at the following site(s):
	*__________Flux.defer ⇢ at org.springframework.ai.openai.OpenAiChatModel.lambda$internalStream$11(OpenAiChatModel.java:351)
	|_   Flux.subscribeOn ⇢ at org.springframework.ai.openai.OpenAiChatModel.lambda$internalStream$11(OpenAiChatModel.java:365)
	*________Flux.flatMap ⇢ at org.springframework.ai.openai.OpenAiChatModel.lambda$internalStream$14(OpenAiChatModel.java:349)

	|_      Flux.doOnNext ⇢ at org.springframework.ai.chat.model.MessageAggregator.aggregate(MessageAggregator.java:105)

Original Stack Trace:
```

I encountered the same issue and have identified the root cause. The problem occurs when processing chunks where the tool call ID is an empty string rather than null. In the OpenAiStreamFunctionCallingHelper class, there's a critical section of code: ```java if (current.toolCalls() != null) { if (current.toolCalls().size() > 1) { throw new IllegalStateException("Currently only one tool call is supported per message!"); } var currentToolCall = current.toolCalls().iterator().next(); if (currentToolCall.id() != null) { // <--- HERE is the problem! This check passes for empty strings if (lastPreviousTooCall != null) { toolCalls.add(lastPreviousTooCall); } toolCalls.add(currentToolCall); } else { toolCalls.add(merge(lastPreviousTooCall, currentToolCall)); // <--- This merging code is skipped for empty strings } } ``` When a chunk contains a tool call with an empty string ID, the condition `currentToolCall.id() != null` evaluates to true (since empty string ≠ null), causing it to execute the wrong branch. This prevents proper merging of the tool calls that should be combined. This eventually leads to the `java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: toolName cannot be null or empty` exception because the function in the chunk doesn't contain the name field information, which would have been available if the tool calls were properly merged.
